fields.label: Cost
fields.help: Cost parameter (1–31, typically 10–14 for login). Higher values increase security and hashing time exponentially.

explanation:
**Technical calculators** for IT: latency, storage, SLA uptime, cloud costs. Calculations that scale your decisions.

## How it works

The cost parameter exponentially affects hashing time through the formula: Time ≈ 2^(cost) × base_time.

This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you if values seem unusual.

## Important notes

This calculation is informational. For critical decisions (security, financial, medical, legal), consult a specialized professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.a: By design. Slowness provides brute-force attack resistance. A fast hash would be vulnerable to password cracking.
Cost 12 is the standard for 2024–2025 login scenarios, targeting ~100–250ms per hash. Adjust based on your acceptable login latency.

faq.q: How does bcrypt compare to Argon2?
faq.a: Argon2 is newer and more resistant to GPU/ASIC attacks. Both are secure; Argon2 is preferred for new systems.
Yes, bcrypt generates and includes a random 16-byte salt automatically with each hash.

faq.q: How do I upgrade the cost for existing passwords?
faq.a: Re-hash on next user login using the new cost parameter. Don't rehash all stored passwords proactively.
